stem: A retail study measures customer satisfaction score across 5 chains, each containing multiple stores. chain A has 20 observations with mean 77.0 /10 (within-chain SD = 8.6). chain B has 10 observations with mean 44.4 /10 (within-chain SD = 8.1). chain C has 15 observations with mean 72.1 /10 (within-chain SD = 9.5). chain D has 5 observations with mean 87.8 /10 (within-chain SD = 5.1). chain E has 15 observations with mean 75.8 /10 (within-chain SD = 5.3). The between-chain standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.7 and 19.2 /10. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true customer satisfaction score for chain A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-chain variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for chain A: 76.9 /10 (shrunk from raw 77.0). Range [76.7, 76.9] as between-chain SD varies from 7.7 to 19.2. Pooled mean: 71.4.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 71.4 /10 (weighted average of 5 chain means).
Shrinkage factor for chain A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=12.8: B = 0.978.
Interval [76.7, 76.9] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (77.0) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (71.4) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
